title: Train a within-lap ranker over drivers in the same race-lap and blend it with the binary model
summary: Pit calls are relative decisions made against the field state, not just absolute thresholds.
rationale: Pit calls are relative decisions made against the field state, not just absolute thresholds. Learning-to-rank objectives are built for grouped binary relevance and can optimize ordering inside each query group; here the natural query is all drivers on the same `(Year,Race,LapNumber)`.
implementation_hint: Use XGBoost `rank:pairwise` or `rank:map` with `qid=(Year,Race,LapNumber)`, label `PitNextLap`, and the current feature set plus field-relative features. Convert per-query ranks to probabilities via OOF calibration, then average or stack with the best binary classifier.
expected_effect: Can improve AUC by emphasizing correct ordering among the handful of plausible pit candidates each lap, especially when class imbalance is severe.
risk: Some lap groups may have few or zero positives, reducing effective pairs; ranking outputs also need careful post-calibration before blending.
